info.title: Neterium Watchlist Screening API
info.description: Highly efficient cloud-based counterparty and transaction screening. To use this API, you will need an authentication token; please contact us to get one.

post.description: Submit one or multiple transactions, as a set of ISO20022-like objects.

parameters.name: collection
parameters.description: The name of the collection you screen against

parameters.name: validate
parameters.description: Should the request be validated?

requestBody.description: The records to screen and the screening options.

post.description: Submit one or multiple names (or texts) with their metadata for screening.

parameters.name: collection
parameters.description: The name of the collection you screen against

parameters.name: validate
parameters.description: Should the request be validated?

requestBody.description: The records to screen and the screening options. Each record should have a name and can have metadata, such as city, country, dob, gender or any other key/value pair you want to give.

get.description: Obtain a session ID to link batches of screening requests together.

post.description: Obtain a session ID to link batches of screening requests together, giving a set of private parameters.

get.description: Retrieves the sessions of your account, with pagination.

parameters.name: skip
parameters.description: The number of sessions to skip before retrieving, 0 by default.

parameters.name: take
parameters.description: The number of sessions to retrieve (page size), 10 by default.

get.description: Retrieves the status and other metadata about the given session.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the session obtained by a call to the /sessions/new endpoint.

put.description: Update the session timer to prevent it to expire.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Obtained by a call to the /sessions/new endpoint or by uploading an ephemeral private list.

put.description: Properly closes your session. If not called, the session will be marked as expired.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the session obtained by a call to the /sessions/new endpoint.

get.description: Gets the description and update status of all the watchlists available in our servers
Gets all the region names in the system. The values can be used in the riskRegions option.
Gets all the known region names for the given country. The values can be used in the riskRegions option.

parameters.name: countryCode
parameters.description: The ISO2 country code

get.description: Gets the complete profile of the specified record id.

parameters.name: profileId
parameters.description: The ID of the profile in Jetscan

get.description: Gets the complete profile of the given list of profiles.

parameters.name: profiles
parameters.description: Comma separated list of profile IDs

post.description: Gets the complete profile of the given list of profiles (max 1000), passed in the body.

200.description: The list of complete profiles, in the same order as the given profile IDs.

post.description: Gets the primary name of the given list of profiles (max 1000), passed in the body.

200.description: The list of primary names, in the same order as the given profile IDs.

get.description: (Deprecated) Gets the complete profile of the specified record id.

parameters.name: profileId
parameters.description: The ID of the profile in Jetscan

get.description: Gets the available sanction codes for a list, along with the sanction name and country.

parameters.name: listId
parameters.description: The ID of the list

get.description: Gets the available sanction codes for a list and country.

parameters.name: listId
parameters.description: The ID of the list

parameters.name: countryCode
parameters.description: The ISO2 country code

get.description: Gets the available category labels for a list.

parameters.name: listId
parameters.description: The ID of the list

get.description: Gets the screening stats for your account, for the current month.

post.description: Gets the screening stats for your account, matching the posted criteria.

get.description: Returns the list of known projects that have been used as projectReference in screening requests

parameters.name: fromDate
parameters.description: The start date of the search period (YYYYMMDD). If omitted, the start date is 30 days in the past.

parameters.name: toDate
parameters.description: The end date of the search period (YYYYMMDD). If omitted, the end date is today.

get.description: Returns the list of known clients that have been used as clientReference in screening requests

parameters.name: fromDate
parameters.description: The start date of the search period (YYYYMMDD). If omitted, the start date is 30 days in the past.

parameters.name: toDate
parameters.description: The end date of the search period (YYYYMMDD). If omitted, the end date is today.

get.description:
The returned HTTP reflects the overall cluster status.
  * 200: The cluster is healthy with at least one collection.
  * 206: The screening is available for at least one collection. Some other API endpoints may not be available.
  * 503: The cluster is not available for the moment or under maintenance.

default.description: One of the bellow response depending on the status

get.description:
The returned HTTP reflects the overall cluster status.
  * 200: The cluster is healthy with at least one collection.
  * 206: The screening is available for at least one collection. Some other API endpoints may not be available.
  * 503: The cluster is not available for the moment or under maintenance.

parameters.name: collection
parameters.description: The name of the collection to check.

default.description: One of the bellow response depending on the status

get.description: Retrieves the presets of your account.

delete.description: Delete multiple presets based on a url query filter

date from which the preset have been created. the date is using the format yyyy-MM-dd
date until which the preset have been created. the date is using the format yyyy-MM-dd
the clientReference that the presets belongs to.
indicate how the name search parameter should be used.

get.description: Gets all the active exceptions of your account, with or without pagination.

parameters.name: skip
parameters.description: The number of exceptions to skip before retrieving, 0 by default.

parameters.name: take
parameters.description: The number of exceptions to retrieve (page size), 100 by default.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: The status of the exception, by default "ACTIVE", "INVALIDATED" and "EXPIRED" are returned

parameters.name: profileId
parameters.description: The id of the related watch list record.

parameters.name: fromDate
parameters.description: the date from which exceptions have been created. use the yyyy-mm-dd format.

parameters.name: toDate
parameters.description: the date until which exceptions have been created. use the yyyy-mm-dd format.

parameters.name: reference
parameters.description: text used when searching exception by reference.

parameters.name: clientReference
parameters.description: The client reference specified when creating the exception.

post.description: Create one or multiple exceptions for your metadata or for specific matches.

206.description: Exceptions partially created (an error happened for some).

delete.description: Delete a list exception, by ID.

parameters.name: ids
parameters.description: A comma-separated list of exception ids

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: The status of the exception, by default all the exceptions are returned

parameters.name: profileId
parameters.description: The id of the related watch list record.

parameters.name: fromDate
parameters.description: the date from which exceptions have been created. use the yyyy-mm-dd format.

parameters.name: toDate
parameters.description: the date until which exceptions have been created. use the yyyy-mm-dd format.

parameters.name: reference
parameters.description: text used when searching exception by reference.

parameters.name: purge
parameters.description: If true, physically delete the exceptions. If false, it sets the status of the exceptions it DELETED. Purged exceptions cannot be recovered.

204.description: The exception is marked as DELETED and will no longer be used.

delete.description: Bulk deletion of exception using url parameter as filters.

schema.description: comma separated list of ids to delete
date from which the exception have been created. the date is using the format yyyy-MM-dd
date until which the exception have been created. the date is using the format yyyy-MM-dd
the clientReference that the exceptions belongs to.

parameters.name: profileId

parameters.name: reference
parameters.description: the reference of the exception

parameters.name: referenceSearchType
parameters.description: indicate how the reference search parameter should be used.

204.description: The exception is marked as DELETED and will no longer be used.

get.description: Retrieves the specified exception, by ID.

parameters.name: exceptionId

put.description: Updates one exception by ID. Only a subset of the properties can be updated (NOT including the core ones, such as profileID or match/source).

parameters.description: The ID of the exception to update

post.description: Upload an anonymous XML private list that will be associated with the provided sessionID, and will never be stored.

parameters.name: classify
parameters.description: Detects the name cultural affinity of the submitted records.

parameters.name: transliterate
parameters.description: Creates a latin alias from the supported native script names.

parameters.name: validate
parameters.description: Validates the uploaded XML file

parameters.name: checksum
parameters.description: MD5 checksum of the uploaded file. Neterium API will compute the checksum and discard the uploaded file if it doesn't match the provided checksum.

parameters.name: includeCities
parameters.description: Should the database of risk cities and regions be also in the scope when screening against this ephemeral list.

requestBody.description: The watch as an XML file, plain or gzipped. The format is the OFAC one (https://www.treasury.gov/ofac/downloads/sdn.xml )

200.description: The private list has been successfully uploaded.

put.description: Upload a delta persistent XML private list (not available in SaaS yet).

parameters.name: listId
parameters.description: Short unique ID of your private list, which will be used to prefix the record IDs internally (along with PRV).

parameters.name: classify
parameters.description: Detects the name cultural affinity of the submitted records.

parameters.name: transliterate
parameters.description: Creates a latin alias from the supported native script names.

parameters.name: validate
parameters.description: Validates the uploaded XML file

parameters.name: clientReference
parameters.description: Additional level of list segregation. Only screening requests containing the same clientReference in the context will be able to match against these records.

parameters.name: checksum
parameters.description: MD5 checksum of the uploaded file. Neterium API will compute the checksum and discard the uploaded file if it doesn't match the provided checksum.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Global action for all records. If the action parameter is not present, the action defined in the record will be used.

requestBody.description: The watch as an XML file, plain or gzipped. The format is the OFAC one (https://www.treasury.gov/ofac/downloads/sdn.xml )

200.description: The private list has been successfully uploaded.

post.description: Upload a persistent XML private list.

parameters.name: listId
parameters.description: Short unique ID of your private list, which will be used to prefix the record IDs internally (along with PRV).

parameters.name: classify

parameters.name: transliterate
parameters.description: Creates a latin alias from the supported native script names.

parameters.name: validate
parameters.description: Validates the uploaded XML file

parameters.name: clientReference
parameters.description: Additional level of list segregation. Only screening requests containing the same clientReference in the context will be able to match against these records.

parameters.name: checksum
parameters.description: MD5 checksum of the uploaded file. Neterium API will compute the checksum and discard the uploaded file if it doesn't match the provided checksum.

requestBody.description: The watch as an XML file, plain or gzipped. The format is the OFAC one (https://www.treasury.gov/ofac/downloads/sdn.xml )

200.description: The private list has been successfully uploaded.

delete.description: Delete a private list and all its records.

parameters.name: listId
parameters.description: Short unique ID of your private list, the same that was used when uploading the list (without the PRV prefix).

204.description: The private list has been successfully deleted.

get.summary: Gets the statistics of a list.
get.description: Gets the statistics of a list, including import date, extracted publication date and the number of records added, updated or deleted during an import, parsing or storing phase.

parameters.name: listId
parameters.description: Short unique ID of the list. For private lists use the prefix PRV.

parameters.name: fromDate
parameters.description: The start date of the search period (YYYYMMDD). If omitted, the start date is the current day.

parameters.name: toDate
parameters.description: The end date of the search period (YYYYMMDD). If omitted, the end date is the current day + one day.

get.summary: Gets the statistics of a list for the specific date.
get.description: Gets the statistics of a list, including import date, extracted publication date and the number of records added, updated or deleted during an import, parsing or storing phase.

parameters.name: listId
parameters.description: short unique ID of the list. For private lists use the prefix PRV.

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: The date of the search (YYYYMMDD).

get.summary: Gets the full report about a list update
get.description: Gets report describing all the steps, status and challenges found during a list update.

parameters.name: listId
parameters.description: short unique ID of the list. For private lists use the prefix PRV.

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: The date of the update

200.description: The reports found for the given list and date. There might be multiple reports if the list is updated more than once per day.

outcome.description: The value will be 'error' in case of error API response

errorCode.description: A numeric error code for easier error classification. We try to always send one, but it might be missing in unexpected cases.

errorMessage.description: Small high-level description of the error.

errorDetails.description: Further details about the cause of the error

CoreApiError.description:
The expected HTTP code will be used depending on the kind of error, following the general REST APIs principles.
  * 400: Bad Request (JSON parsing or validation error, ...)
  * 401: Not Authorized (You do not have a valid account in your token)
  * 403: Forbidden (You are trying to access a resource you are not allowed to use; e.g. an endpoint, an API feature, a list, ...)
  * 404: Not Found (The requested resource does not exist)
  * 409: Conflict (The resource you are trying to create conflicts with an existing one; e.g. with the same unique name)
  * 500: Unexpected server error (We recommend to contact our support in this case if the problem persists)
  * 503: Service Unavailable (The service is currently under maintenance and will be available soon)

example.errorDetails: You need a token to access this API
You need a token to access this API
You do not have access to this collection
Profile not found in the watch list repository.
You have exceeded your screening quota.
The screening service is temporarly unavailable, please submit your request again.

outcome.description: The value should be 'success' in this case.

count.description: Number of records in the data property

outcome.description: The value should be 'success' in this case.
