outcome.description: The value should be 'success' in this case.

count.description: Number of records in the data property

outcome.description: The value should be 'success' in this case.

count.description: Number of records in the data property

total.description: Total number of records in the queried dataset

skip.description: Number of skipped records in the queried dataset (should match the 'skip' parameter in the initial query)

accountId.description: ID of the account this session belongs to.

reference.description: If any, the reference that was given when the session was created.

projectReference.description: If any, the projectReference that was given when the session was created.

clientReference.description: If any, the clientReference that was given when the session was created.

startDate.description: Date at which the session has started (date-time - RFC3339).

stopDate.description: Date at which the session ended - completed, aborted or expired - (date-time - RFC3339).

updateDate.description: Date at which the session was updated.

perfs.description: Some performance stats. Measures the number of screened records and produced hits every 3 seconds (approx).

checksum.description: MD5 checksum of the uploaded file

sessionId.description: An optional session Id this request refers to. See the session API.

clientReference.description: An optional client Id for this batch. Can also be given when creating the session or at record level.

projectReference.description: An optional project Id for this batch. Can also be given when creating the session or at record level.

reference.description: Your optional unique identifier for this query.

outcome.description: The status of the operation

count.description: Number of returned sessions (always 1 in this case)

data.description: The session created after a successful list upload

outcome.description: The status of the operation

count.description: Number of private list (always 1 in this case)

data.description: The session created after a successful list upload

PrivateListUploadResult.title: The result of the load of a private list

version.description: Publication date of the list (date-time - RFC3339).

invalidRecords.description: The count of invalid records (XML validation)

threshold.description:
Name matching threshold.
When the threshold value is between 50 and 84 matching is more broad, and some hits returned in this mode, are not retrieved otherwise

matching.description:
Matching technique:
  + `fuzzy` is the default sensitivity in terms of fuzzy logic capabilities and the affinity specific strategies.
  + `strict` (obsolete) applies a higher penalty for missing and mismatching words, while keeping the same level of fuzziness between words.
  + `balanced` (obsolete) only accepts a shorter set of misspells (doubled letters, letter inversions, mismatched vowels) and all the important words need to be matched, but the screening speed is extremely fast. 
  + `broad` is a Google-like search that raises for example hits when searching for a last name only. Note that the threshold is not taken into account for this setting.
  
When screening transactions, we assume that only the "fuzzy" option is applicable. The balanced and strict values will be removed from the API in the next major release.

recordType.description: By default, a screened record is of type 'unknown' (i.e. "individual" or "entity") in Jetscan and 'transaction' in JetFlow. With this option, you can set the default value of all the records in a Jetscan request, or change the value of a Jetflow field using fieldPresets. However, please note that the 'transaction' recordType is reserved for JetFlow queries and cannot be used in field- and tag- presets.

metaScore.description: When enabled, the final score will also take into account any matched metadata, assuming that the requested precision is reached. For example, if the DOB precision is set to 5 years, the closer they match, the higher the score; but the hit still be discarded if the dob difference is still > 5 years.

geoScore.description: (JETFLOW Only) When enabled, the API will search all the transaction fields for matches with the countries of any reported hit. As a result, a new score will be delivered in the response.

geoThreshold.description: (JETFLOW Only, requires geoScore option enabled) Defines a minimum threshold for the geoScore in the found matches. If the compute geoScore is below this threshold, the matches will not be reported.

bicThreshold.description: The threshold to be used when screening expanded BICs. By default, the top level threshold is used instead. Setting a value to 0 will disable automatic BIC expansion.

preset.description:
Name of the options preset to use. The preset values will overwrite any other option passed in this object.
Note that another preset cannot be referenced using this option.

lowQualityAliases.description: Specifies what kind of lowQualityAlias should be included in the screening scope. For the moment, only the reclassified aliases can be included.

dobMatching.description: DOB matching type: 'perfect' to match the full date if available, 'year' to accept the match if it's the same year (or near, see dobPrecision).

dobPrecision.description: DOB matching precision: in case of year matching, the accepted difference (in years). Note: when metaScore is set to “true”, and if the dob is within the defined range set by this parameter, the calculation of the score will take into account the ratio (actual year difference) / (dobPrecision ). For example, a difference of 3 years between 2 dates with dobPrecision =7 years will score higher than if dobPrecision = 4 years for the same 3 year difference.

idPrecision.description: Required number of consecutive matching characters in two compared IDs in order to be consirered as potential matches.

tags.description:
List of profile tags restrictions (high level Jetscan specific categories). Put a ! in front of a tag to create an exclusion condition. For sanction (or payment) screening, we recommended to use SAN and LOC.

  List types:

    * `SAN` - Sanctions Lists 
    * `SIE` = Special Interest Entity
    * `SIP` = Special Interest Person
    * `PEP` = Politically Exposed Person
    * `RCA` = Relative or Close Associate
    * `SOC` = State Owned Company
    * `OOL` = Other Official Lists
    * `AM` = Adverse Media
    * `BRD` = Board Member
    * `SCO` = Sanctions Control & Ownership

  Entity types:

    * `LOC` = Risk Country
    * `VSL` = Vessels
    * `AIR` = Aircrafts

categories.description: List of profile categories to match. The possible values depend on the list vendor. The selection does match if at least ONE of the elements is matched (OR). If an element is an array, ALL the sub-elements should be matched (AND).

sanctionCountries.description: List of iso 2 country codes defining which sanctions has to be searched.

sanctions.description: List of codes defining which sanctions has to be searched. The list of available codes depends on the watch list provider.

privateLists.description: List of private list IDs that are to be used in the screening scope. Only applicable when screening against the 'private' collection. The values in this option should not contain the PRV prefix.

matchTypes.description: List of match types (see the type field in the raised matches) to be included in the scope. You can use a ! character to exclude the item instead.

scopeFilter.description: A expression which should evaluate as a boolean, indicating if the corresponding profile should be considered as a potential match. It is evaluated AFTER the above individual scoping options (tags, categories, sanctions, sanctionCountries).
scopeFilter.example: ["profile.tags == 'SAN'","(profile.tags != 'VSL' and profile.catogories == 'CATEGORY1') or (sanctions == 'ABC')","profile.sanctions in ['CODE1', 'CODE2', 'CODE3'] and profile.tags != 'RCA'"]

cityProximity.description: Accepted distance (km) between two mismatched cities. Note that if metaScore is set to “true”, and if the location is within the defined range set by this parameter, the calculation of the score will take into account the ratio (actual distance) / (cityProximity). For example, a distance of 100km between 2 locations with cityProximity =1000 km will score higher than if cityProximity = 200 km for the same distance.

charset.description: Character set used in your queries. Set it to auto if you are sending original script names.

affinity.description:
Cultural affinity of the screened name. Depending on the specified (or detected) affinity some specific matching rules may be used to order to improve the quality of the output. [def] is the default (latin based) affinity, the engine will use the default settings, but after a transliteration happens the appropriate affinity will be applied. [off] will disabled this feature completely and use the default latin affinity even after a transliteration. [auto] will make the engine try to guess the cultural affinity of the screened name, at least a country in the 'places' metadata is recommended to increase to accuracy of the prediction. [trusted] will use the affinity detected in the watchlist record instead, which is useful if the input data is incomplete or potentially incorrect. The other values are ISO country codes that describe (the best) the name's cultural affinity. In case of mismatch between the guessed affinity and the one of the potential match the default rules are used instead. Please note that both the AI model and custom rule sets behind this feature as well as the list of supported affinities will keep evolving in the future.

  Language Codes:
  + ar = Arabic
  + cn = Chinese 
  + kr = Korean
  + ru = Russian

geoPolicy.description: List of setups that will define which risk countries and regions should be alertable and with which conditions (languages, city types and population)

riskCountries.description:
List of iso 2 risk country codes defining the countries against which you should match your data. 
Potential matches are country and city names in your screened object (in TEXT mode) and places in the metadata.
***Planned deprecation: Nov-2025, use geoPolicy***

riskRegions.description:
List of regions to identify, including regions from private lists, in which main cities should be spotted in the requests. 
If the region exists in several countries, specify the country associated with the city by adding "/isoCode" . 
Check the repository endpoints to get the list of all the available region values.
***Planned deprecation: Nov-2025, use geoPolicy***

anyOf.description: (This version will be obsoleted, please contact us for details) Minimum status of a matched record. If 'active', only the active profiles are matched. If 'inactive', active and inactive profiles are matched. if 'deceased' only deceased profiles are matched.
List of record status. To be matched, any potential profile should have its status in that list. If the list is empty (or not given), any status is accepted.

tagStatus.description: The requested status of the tag, when specified in a tagPreset (this has no effect when used in the top-level options). If 'active', only profiles with the selected tag marked as active are matched. If 'inactive', only profiles with the selected tag marked as inactive are matched. If 'both', profiles with selected marked as active or inactive are matched. This option is typically used in SAN or PEP tagPresets, as those tags have a dedicated status assigned by the list provider.

inactiveDays.description: Number of days since this record was flagged as inactive (PEP or RCA); available only in some watch lists.

pepLevel.description: For PEPs, the minimum level of responsability

explainMatch.description: Profile ID of a target you want a query plan for. If specified, the matches are not returned.

explainLocation.description: (Jetflow Only) Path to the transaction field in which the hit is expected. See the location property in the screening response.

additionalCollections.description: List of other collections to query. The results will be merged with the screening output of the current collection.

exclusions.description: Array of (root level) JetFlow property names which should NOT be screened. By default, it includes paymentId, amount, paymentType, direction, transactionId, transactionType. paymentId and paymentType have been replaced by transactionId and transactionType and are now obsolete.

definitions.description: If you want to embed a preset and reference it in multiple fields, you can declare them here.

fieldPresets.description: By default the top level options and presets are used. This object can overwrite this and tell which preset is to be used with which field.

tagPresets.description: Ordered mapping between a tag found in the potentially matched watchlist record and a preset to use. The first applicable mapping is used. Options in a mapped preset will overwrite options (or presets) in the root options block. Please note that, because of a current limitation, record selection options cannot be used with a LOC tagPreset (such as categories, lastUpdated, privateLists, sanctions and sanctionCountries), this will be improved in a future release.

take.description: Maximum number of matches to retrieve per screened record from the result set (used for paging).

skip.description: Number of matches to skip from the result set per screened record (used for paging).

lastUpdated.description:
This option allows to screen records against a delta of the watchlist. 
Enter a (past) date and only the records updated from that date until now have the potential to match. 
Date format can be precise date (YYYY-MM-DD or YYYYMMDD) or an integer (0 = today, -1 = yesterday, etc).
The support for this option depends on the watchlist provider, please contact us for more information.

anyOf.description: The date as a (negative) number of days in the past

minSourceAge.description:
Minimum publication date of the most recent source attached to the matched record. 
You can use this option to exclude watchlist records that do not come with recent publications / press releases.

anyOf.description: The date as a (negative) number of days in the past

detectDuplicates.description: (Jetflow) will include this transaction in a duplicate detection process and return a status if the exact same one has been seen before.

detectStripping.description: (Jetflow) will include this transaction in a stripping detection process and return a status if an attempt to resubmit a similar transaction without the reported hit(s) is identified.

CorePresetName.description: The name of the preset to apply when screening this property

CorePresetFields.description: An array of field names in the transaction, the key in the fieldPresets is then supposed to be a preset name. This is a convenient way to assign a preset to many fields.

clientReference.description: Your client ID if you need one. If you want this value to impact the screening stats repartition, you currently have to use if in the request's context bloc instead. This will be improved in a future release.

projectReference.description: Your project ID if you need one. If you want this value to impact the screening stats repartition, you currently have to use if in the request's context bloc instead. This will be improved in a future release.

name.description: (Obsolete) The screened name. In case of individual name, the expected format is [surname(s)], [first name] [middle name(s)]. Please use 'object' instead in order to be ready when it will be removed from the API.

object.description: The screened data. In case of individual name, the expected format is [surname(s)], [first name] [middle name(s)].

type.description: Always try to give one, it will improve the accuracy and the speed. Type 'unknown' is treated as a name that is either 'individual'  or 'entity').

notes.description: Additional list provider specific notes about this date. (e.g. Past)

prefix.description: Prefix, if the list is giving one.

suffix.description: Suffix, if the list is giving one.

quality.description: Level of quality of the alias, if known by the list provider.

type.description: Type of name, mainly used for aliases, but it will be AKA in most cases. (PRI: Primary name, AKA: Also Known As, OSN: Original Script Name, MDN: Maiden Name, ALT: Alternate Spelling)

outcome.description: The value should be 'success' in this case.

count.description: Number of records in the data property

_id.description: Unique identifier of the profile in the Jetscan repository (16 characters). This is always composed by a list prefix and then the ID in that list.

timestamp.description: Timestamp of the profile's last update (date-time - RFC3339)

notes.description: Other other non structured data given by the list

description.description: Optional verbose description or additional information about this ID.

issuer.description: ISO2 country code where this ID has been issued.

outcome.description: The value should be 'success' in this case.

count.description: Number of records in the results property

clientReference.description: Your client ID if you need one

projectReference.description: Your project ID if you need one

outcome.description: The value should be 'success' in this case.

count.description: Number of records in the data property

outcome.description: The value should be 'success' in this case.

count.description: Number of records in the data property

key.description: Short ID of the list, used as prefix in the profile IDs.

source.description: URL from which the data is downloaded.

description.description: More verbose description of the list.

moreInfo.description: URL to the list web site.

lastUpdated.description: Last time this list was downloaded by our system (date-time - RFC3339).

lastPublished.description: Last time this list was updated and published by the provider (date-time - RFC3339).

size.description: Total number of records in this list.

outcome.description: The value should be 'success' in this case.

count.description: Number of records in the data property

outcome.description: The value should be 'success' in this case.

count.description: Number of records in the data property

code.description: The sanction code, that can be used in the 'sanctions' option of any screening request.

country.description: ISO country code of the country in which this sanction is applicable. 'xi' is used for international sanctions.

listId.description: The ID of the list this sanction belongs to.

category.description: A sanction category, if available (rarely used)

labels.description: List of sanction names. There's normally only one name, but some consolidated sanctions do regroup a list of official publications (e.g. EU list)

listId.description: The ID of the list this sanction belongs to.

category.description: The exact category label, which can be used in the 'categories' option of any screening request.

month.description: The month you want the stats for (YYYY/MM/DD). If omitted, the current month is used. The day part is mandatory but will not be used.

client.description: the ID of your client, as sent in the screening requests.

project.description: the ID of your project, as sent in the screening requests.

fromDate.description: The starting date of the period to use for the data extraction (YYYY/MM/DD). If omitted, the beginning of time is used.

toDate.description: The end date of the period to use for the data extraction (YYYY/MM/DD). If omitted, all the records after the fromDate are returned.

collection.description: Collection for which the query should be executed. By default, all the accessible collection stats are returned.

amount.description: Amount of money, use the dot as decimal separator.

initiatingParty.description: The party on the initiative of which the payment data is established. This might be the payer itself, an agent, or the company service center.

debtor.description: The party whose account is debited with the payment.

debtorAgent.description: The party which is the bank of the payer.

ultimateDebtor.description: The party that originally ordered goods or services and to whom the seller has send the invoice. Ultimate debtor is used when the receiver of the invoice is different than the payer.

creditor.description: The party whose account is credited with the payment.

creditorAgent.description: The party which is the bank of the beneficiary.

ultimateCreditor.description: The party which is the ultimate beneficiary of the payment. For example, the payment is credited to an account of a financing company, but the ultimate beneficiary is the customer of the financing company.

items.description: Other financial institutions involved in the transaction chain.

purpose.description: Underlying reason for the transaction (screened as text).

JetFlowProperty.description:
Any custom string property

We recommend that property names are:
  + Camel-cased, ascii strings.
  + The first character must be a letter, or an underscore (_) if it is excluded from screening
  + Subsequent characters can be a letter, a digit, or an underscore

JetFlowTextParty.description: Unstructured party with all the data in a single string. Expect a higher rate of false positives when using this version.

label.description: Optional label to further help the sender identifying the party's role, or mapping it to the original transation object.

items.description: Unique ID for this institution or agent

name.description: Full name of the party. In case of individual, it's better to user the SURNAME, FIRSTNAME MIDDLENAMES format when applicable. 'name' and 'text' are not expected to be sent within the same party.

text.description: In case of unstructured data, put your text to be screened here. Same as directly using the text version of a Party.

postalAddress.description: (Deprecated, please use the postalAddresses field instead) Party addresses.

relatedCountries.description:
Free key/value pairs of any other related country (nationalities,
citizenhips, country of jurisdiction...). Only the ISO country code in the pair's
value will be used.

We recommend that property names are:
  + Camel-cased, ascii strings.
  + The first character must be a letter, or an underscore (_) if it is excluded from screening
  + Subsequent characters can be a letter, a digit, or an underscore

dob.description: Date of birth of this individual, if applicable. Supported formats are YYYY or YYYY/MM/DD.

pob.description: Place of birth of this individual.

gender.description: For individual parties, the gender of the person.

type.description: Specifies the nature, or use of the account.

currency.description: Identification of the currency in which the account is held (ISO Currency code).

name.description: Name of the account, as assigned by the account servicing institution, in agreement with the account owner in order to provide an additional means of identification of the account.

proxy.description: Specifies an alternate assumed name for the identification of the account.

JetFlowTextAddress.description: Full text version of the address.

country.description: ISO country code. Required when using the structured version of the address.

text.description: Unstructured version of the address. The system will extract any city/country pair. If not empty, this field has priority over the other structured fields.

id.description: Unique identifier of the preset, given in the creation response.

name.description: Your unique name of the preset. To be used in screening requests to reference it.

options.description: The set of options of this preset.

clientReference.description: If you are multi-tenant, the ID of the client owning this preset. Only screening requests with the same client reference will have access to it.

userReference.description: Any user reference to be used in the audit log. When omitted, the accountId will be used instead.

accountId.description: Your accountId. No need to send this field.

outcome.description: The value should be 'success' in this case.

count.description: Number of records in the data property

items.description: Primary name of the corresponding received profile.

outcome.description: The value should be 'success' in this case.

count.description: Number of records in the data property

outcome.description: The value should be 'success' in this case.

count.description: Number of records in the data property

id.description: Internal exception id, to be used for update and delete operations.

clientReference.description: If you are multi-tenant, the ID of the client owning this exception. Only screening requests with the same client reference will have access to it.

userReference.description: Any user reference to be used in the audit log. When omitted, the accountId will be used instead.

profileId.description: Profile ID of the watch list record attached to this exception.

match.description: Name (or Alias) of the watch list record which should be accepted. When empty, any match with the given profile will be managed by this exception.

source.description: Exact string from which the match has been triggered. The exception will only be active if the same source is screened again (not case sensitive). This data is hashed and will not be kept on our servers. When empty, any string triggering the specified match will be managed by this exception.

expirationType.description: Determines in which condition this exception should be invalidated: never (the default), date (scheduled), profile (when the profile changes in the watch list).

expirationDate.description: If the expirationType is set to DATE, the expiration date of the exception. By default, the exception expires at 00:00:00 UTC on expirationDate

sourceHash.description: Internal hash of the source data, as the real valueu is not stored on our servers.

matchHash.description: Internal hash of the matched name, if any.

profileChecksum.description: Profile checksum (as given in the screening results) to be used if the expirationType is set to PROFILE.

coreChecksum.description: Core Profile checksum (as given in the screening results) to be used if the expirationType is set to CORE_PROFILE.

prefix.description: Optional prefix which should be found before the screened source in order to apply this exception.

suffix.description: Optional suffix which should be found before the screened source in order to apply this exception.

conditions.description: Additional restrictions on the request or found match for this exception to be applicable.

created.description: Creation date of this exception. (date-time - RFC3339).

updated.description: Last update date of this exception. (date-time - RFC3339).

CoreException.description: An object describing a match, or a specific profile, that should be automatically discarded based on some given conditions

CoreExceptionBody.description: Payload of an exception creation request

CoreExceptionUpdateBody.description: Payload of an exception update request

type.description: An optional exception type you can give, that will also be returned in the screening results if the exception is activated.

clientReference.description: If you are multi-tenant, the ID of the client owning this exception. Only screening requests with the same client reference will have access to it.

userReference.description: Any user reference to be used in the audit log. When omitted, the accountId will be used instead.

profileId.description: Profile ID of the watch list record attached to this exception. *Note that this property cannot be updated for an existing* exception.

match.description: Name (or Alias) of the watch list record which should be accepted. When empty, any match with the given profile will be managed by this exception. *Note that this property cannot be updated for an existing exception.*

source.description: Exact string from which the match has been triggered. The exception will only be active if the same source is screened again (not case sensitive). This data is hashed and will not be kept on our servers. When empty, any string triggering the specified match will be managed by this exception. Note that this property cannot be updated for an existing exception.

expirationType.description: Determines in which condition this exception should be invalidated: never (the default), date (scheduled), profile (when the profile changes in the watch list).

expirationDate.description: If the expirationType is set to DATE, the expiration date of the exception. supported formats are date such as '2025-05-01' or date time such as '2025-05-01T10:10:00Z. see rfc3339 section 5.6 for more details.

profileChecksum.description: Profile checksum (as given in the screening results) to be used if the expirationType is set to PROFILE.

coreChecksum.description: Core Profile checksum (as given in the screening results) to be used if the expirationType is set to CORE_PROFILE.

prefix.description: Optional prefix which should be found before the screened source in order to apply this exception.

suffix.description: Optional suffix which should be found after the screened source in order to apply this exception.

conditions.description: Additional restrictions on the request or found match for this exception to be applicable.

notBefore.description: Date from which the exception will be applied. The date must follow the ISO-8601 standard and will be saved as a UTC

CoreExceptionRequest.description: An object describing a match, or a specific profile, that should be automatically discarded based on some given conditions

clientReference.description: If you are multi-tenant, the ID of the client owning this exception. Only screening requests with the same client reference will have access to it.

userReference.description: Any user reference to be used in the audit log. When omitted, the accountId will be used instead.

expirationType.description: Determines in which condition this exception should be invalidated: never (the default), date (scheduled), profile (when the profile changes in the watch list).

expirationDate.description: If the expirationType is set to DATE, the expiration date of the exception.

profileChecksum.description: Profile checksum (as given in the screening results) to be used if the expirationType is set to PROFILE.

prefix.description: Optional prefix which should be found before the screened source in order to apply this exception.

suffix.description: Optional suffix which should be found after the screened source in order to apply this exception.

conditions.description: Additional restrictions on the request or found match for this exception to be applicable.

CoreExceptionUpdateRequest.description: An object containing the fields to update in an existing exception

anyOf.description: Key/Value pairs where the key is the path to a field in either the metadata, the location or the profile summary, and the value is the string (or a list of possible strings) to match with that extracted field. From the request, the following fields are available - /context - /metadata (jetscan only) - /reference (jetscan only) - /object (jetflow only) - /transactionId, /transactionType, /currency, /amount for Jetflow From the response, the following fields are available - /match
Array variant of the same object, with a list of property paths and expected value(s). The array variant can also include an operator.

path.description: Path to the property to extract

anyOf.description: The list of accepted values for the extracted field.

One single element of type string is also allowed The value is considered to be a regular expression if it starts with the special character ~. example ~[0-9]{1,2} The value is replaced with the content of a path (extracted value) when wrapped in ${} such as ${/match/profileId}

operator.description: the type of comparison operator to apply on the path such as =, !=, >, >=, <, <=, between, contains

countries.description: List of iso 2 risk country codes defining the countries against which you should match your data.

regions.description: List of regions to identify, including regions from private lists, in which main cities should be spotted in the requests. If the region exists in several countries, specify the country associated with the city by adding "/isoCode" . Check the repository endpoints to get the list of all the available region values.

languages.description:
List of language codes that defines the accepted cities and countries translations (when available); the native and international spellings 
are always active.
If you want to screen against any supported translation, leave this field empty.

cityLevels.description: The feature class of a city (PPLA stands for any PPLA1 to PPLA5 class)

minPopulation.description: Sets the minimum population of a city.

threshold.description: The threshold to automatically apply to any match with cities belonging to this policy. By default, the threshold of the screening request is used.

value.description:
Tells if the metadata value SHOULD be defined in the watch list record
in order to accept a hit. With EXPECTED, a small penalty is applied to the final score in case of missing data while with OPTIONAL the score is left untouched.  
*Note that, when the metaScore is enabled, the default value of the DOB, CITY, COUNTRY policies is 'expected' instead of 'optional'.*

match.description:
Tells if the metadata value (when present), SHOULD match the one
in your record (when present) in order to accept a hit.  
*Note that, when the metaScore is enabled, the default value is 'optional' instead of 'required'.*

level.description: If the metadata value is found and matched, tells the level of importance you give the hit.

mismatchImpact.description: (Advanced) With metaScore enabled, sets the maximum malus points to deduct from final score when this data point does not match (outside of requested precision)

matchImpact.description: (Advanced) With metaScore enabled, sets the maximum bonus to add to the final score when this data point is matching (within the requested precision)

noDataImpact.description: (Advanced) With metaScore enabled, sets the maximum malus points to deduct from final score when this datapoint is missing the watchlist profile

version.description: Version of the Jetscan screening service. Keep this if you want to be able to replay a request later.

api.description: Version of the API schema implemented in this release.

lists.description: Key properties and update status of the lists used in the collection.

reference.description: Your optional request reference, given in the request context.

projectReference.description: Your optional project reference, given in the request context.

clientReference.description: Your optional client reference, given in the request context.

accountId.description: Your Neterium account ID, extracted from your token.

collection.description: The collection you have queried in the URL.

projectReference.description: The project reference, if given in the request's context.

clientReference.description: The client reference, if given in the request's context.

CoreResponseContext.description: Screening context, including the input context as long as the list and software versions.

elapsed.description: Number of milliseconds it took to screen the posted requests on the server side. The format will always be "xxxx ms".

hitRecords.description: Number of screened records with at least one match

recoveredRecords.description: Number of screened records for which all matches are discarded by an exception

errorRecords.description: Number of records which could not be screened

hits.description: Total number of found matches in this batch, inclusing the discarded ones, regardless of the responseContent/discardedMatches option). Note that, when explainLocation is used, it's the total number of hits for the given path.

discardedHits.description: Number of hits discarded by an exception

hitPercent.description: Percentage of records with at least one match

screenedPerSecond.description: Number of records screened per second on the server side

consumedQuota.description: Indication (in percentage) of the API usage compared to the quota granted in your account based on the token volume cycle (e.g. daily / monthly / annual).

CoreResponseStats.description: Some stats about the screening process.

_index.description: Position of this record in your request

object.description: The screened name/text (omitted in case of transaction).

matchCount.description: Number of matches from the result set for the screened record (less than or equal to value set for take). This should be equal to the length of the 'matches' array. Note that, depending on responseContent/discardedMatches = true/false, this count includes/excludes the number of discardedMatches.

discardedCount.description: Number of matches which have been discarded because of an exception

skipped.description: Number of matches skipped in this search (if 'skip' was given in the search request's options).

taken.description: Number of matches taken in this search (if 'take' was given in the search request's options).

error.description: This field indicates there was an error while screening this particular record. However, when present, the overall HTTP status is still 'success' as the whole batch cannot be rejected just because of a very specific error in 1 query.

matchId.description: A unique identifier for this match, given by Neterium.

profileId.description: Unique ID of the matched entity in our repository

profileUrl.description: URL you can use to get the full entity profile

suffix.description: Suffix of the matched name (if available).

screened.description: The text in the request in which the match was found. When screening names, it should be the same as the input string, when screening texts it will be a snippet containing the matched name.

score.description: Level of confidence of the match, or the full meta score if requested.

geoScore.description: Gives an indication about the level of clues found in the whole transaction, that matches any of the countries which are relevant for this hit. A value of 0 means that no evidence has been found. A value above 50 means that at least one country reference as been found. More references will further increase the score.

geoClues.description: List of geographic clues found in the screened transaction, that match one of the countries of the matched profile. A clue is a reference to a field in the transaction, a country iso code and an optional city name. E.g. '/debtor/ru/moscow'

level.description: The strength of the hit based on the elements that have matched (name, city, country, gender, dob, id), or if level is “discarded”, the match was ignored because of an exception. If only name matches and no other metadata, the level is always "low". Otherwise, the level corresponds to the highest level assigned to the matched criteria as defined in the metadata policies.

scoreScope.description: When the metascore is activated, this field contains the list of matched attributes.

name.description: Primary name of the matched profile

suffix.description: Company suffix if separately specified by the list provider

tags.description:
Assigned tags (PEP, SAN, ...)

  List types:

    * `SAN` - Sanctions Lists 
    * `SIE` = Special Interest Entity
    * `SIP` = Special Interest Person
    * `PEP` = Politically Exposed Person
    * `RCA` = Relative or Close Associate
    * `SOC` = State Owned Company
    * `OOL` = Other Official Lists
    * `AM` = Adverse Media
    * `BRD` = Board member
    * `SCO` = Sanctions Control & Ownership

  Entity types:

    * `LOC` = Risk Country
    * `VSL` = Vessels
    * `AIR` = Aircrafts

inactiveTags.description: List of tags explicitely marked as inactive by the list provider

categories.description: The list of categories assigned to this profile in the watch list

sanctions.description: The unique code of all the sanction lists in which this profile appears. Those codes can be used in the 'sanctions' option.

status.description: Is this profile still active in its watchlist (Active, Inactive, Deceased, Unknown) ?

timestamp.description: Timestamp of the profile's last update (date-time - RFC3339)

ids.description: List of all the IDs found in the profile, which can potentially be matched with the ids sent in the screened metadata.

checksum.description: A hash of the whole watch list profile so that you can check in your alerts database if it has changed or not.

coreChecksum.description: Profile 'footprint' (CRC) which will remain unchanged except if any 'important' information has changed. Important information includes all the properties that are part on the screening logic in a way or an other, including names, addresses, ids, dates of birth, sanctions, status, ...

CoreResponseProfileSummary.description: Additional information about the matched profile

path.description: Field where the hit was found. For JetScan, the value is always '/' while it's the screened field in a JetFlow transaction (e.g. '/debtor/name').

from.description: The 0-based start position in the screened TEXT where the match was found.

to.description: The 0-based end position in the screened TEXT where the match was found.

charset.description: If the match is produced by a transliterated name, the detected charset (see the 'charset' option).

CoreResponseLocation.description: An identication of where the match was found, in a complex screened object (text or transaction).

reference.description: Your exception reference, given when the exception has been created.

CoreResponseException.description: When present, this tells which exception was found and applied on this match.

CoreResponsePlan.description: In the case you requested an explained plan by using the explainMatch option, this will contain a detailed text description about each step of the screening process. The content of this text plan may change with the version of the screening logic itself and is not linked to the version of the API.

appliedAffinity.description:
If the name affinity is enabled in the options (either forced or auto detected), this is the one that was applied when scoring the match.

Language Codes:
  + ar = Arabic
  + cn = Chinese 
  + gr = Greek
  + it = Italian
  + jp = Japanese
  + kr = Korean
  + ru = Russian
  + es = Hispanic
  + vn = Vietnamese

score.description: Level of confidence of the match, or the full meta score if requested.

nameMatching.description: List of matching details about each word in the watch list name.

CoreResponseMatchDetails.description: In the case you requested an explained plan by using the explainMatch option, this will contain a JSON explanation of the score calculation.

potentialImpact.description: Maximum flat impact on the final score, based on the outcome and the level.

impact.description: The flat value that is added (or substracted) from the name score based on the outcome, the level and the score.

level.description: The weight type of this metadata criteria, which will determine how big the final impact will be. This value can be set in the corresponding 'policy' section in the request options.

distance.description: For city matching, the distance in km; for dob matching, the distance in years. A distance of 0 means a perfect match (based on the policy).

outcome.description: Tells if this metadata has been matched or not. The 'incomplete' value means that the matched profile did not contain a corresponding value that could be compared to the one sent in the request. 'not applicable' is returned if this metadata was simply not sent in the request and thus not used in the metascore calculation.

matchValue.description: If any, the corresponding metadata value in the watchlist profile.

requestValue.description: If any, the metadata value from the screening request.

score.description: A matching percentage value based on the distance.

bics.description: List of expanded BIC codes found in the screened object

places.description: List of extracted places. They can come from any screened TEXT, from metadata places or transaction's addresses.

CoreResponseResolvedContent.description: List of structured and typed data extracted from the input record

sanctionProximityScore.description: A score which gives a risk assessment of the proximity of this profile from an sanctioned one. Possible values are 100 (sanctioned profile), 80 (in direct relationship with a sanctioned profile), 50 (second level of relationship), 0 (no relation found).

countryHeatMap.description: A key/value store containing the number of references to a country found in this profile and all its relationships.

mediaAgeScore.description: A score giving an indication of the age of the adverse media articles found in this profile.

mediaQuantityScore.description: A score giving an indication of the amount of adverse media articles found in this profile, compared to the average number in the whole database.

CoreResponseAnalytics.description: Additional analytics recorded by Neterium for this profile.

outcome.description: Should be 'success', as the status request could be processed.

status.description: Overall status of the cluster. The yellow status means that at least one screener service is running but other component(s) are currently having issues.

message.description: More comprehensive text about the status of the cluster

healthyScreeners.description: Number of screening services which are currently ready to receive traffic

CoreClusterStatus.description: A summary of the status of the key screening cluster components

reference.description: Unique reference of the test case

category.description: Category or group id of the test case

scenario.description: Subcategory, variation type or variation code

expected.description: Expected screening result for the provided profile id (hit by default)

profileId.description: Profile id that is expected to generate a hit or nohit result

locationPath.description: Record field that contains the test name

status.description: Status of the validation. passed = Test case meets the expected result, i.e. a hit is raised against the given profile id if a hit is expected, or no hit is raised if no hit is expected. failed = Test case does not meet the expected result, i.e. a hit is found against the given profile id although no hit is expected, or no hit is found although a hit is expected. skipped = Test case cannot be run, e.g. due to an exception during the screening.

outcome.description: The value should be 'success' in this case.

count.description: Number of records in the data property

data.description: List of reports for a list.

outcome.description: The value should be 'success' in this case.

count.description: Number of records in the data property

data.description: List of statistics for a list.

listSanction.description: When applicable, the sanction for which the update was triggered. (SWIFT Only)

outcome.description: Overall status of the list update. In case of failure, the report should contains more details about what went wrong.

timestamp.description: Date and time (RFC3339) at which the update was triggered.

mode.description: Update mode (full or delta). The value is set to unknown if the update process did fail.

recordCount.description: Number of records found in the file

coverageIssues.description: Number of issues found during the coverage phase. Should normally be 0. See the coverage section for more details.

mappingIssues.description: Number of issues found during the mapping phase. Should ideally be 0 but errors can happen. See the parsing section for more details.

sessionId.description: Unique identifier of this update session

startTime.description: Date and time at which the update process started

endTime.description: Date and time at which the update process ended

datafile.title: Detailed information about the downloaded file and the mapping process

archiveName.description: Archive file name, if applicable. The file will be uncompressed before being parsed.

archiveSize.description: Size of the downloaded archive, in bytes

fileSize.description: Size of the uncompressed parsed file, in bytes

recordCount.description: Number of records parsed in the file

mappingIssues.description: List of issues found during the mapping process. Example of issues are : unknown country name, invalid date formats, invalid BIC or ISIN codes, wrong email addresses, etc. Those issues are usually not blocking and the list provider should be contacted in case of recurring problems.

recordId.description: Unique identifier of the record in which the issue was found

resolution.description: Description of what has been done with the wrong field

location.description: Name of the field in which the issue was found

recordCount.description: Total number of records in the database for this list, after the update is applied.

updateCount.description: Actual number of modifications made to the database (including deletes).

updateLog.description: Effect of the applied updates to the database

modified.description: Actual number of updated records. If the record in the file is the same as the record in the database, no update will happen.

deleted.description: Actual number of deleted records. If the record was no more in the database, no delete will happen.

unchanged.description: Number of records that did not change (same checksum after the list update)

createdProfiles.title: IDs of all the records that have been actually added to the database

updatedProfiles.title: IDs of all the records that have been actually updated in the database

deletedProfiles.title: IDs of all the records that have been actually deleted from the database

totalNames.description: Total number of names (incl. aliases) that were found in the parsed file.

checkedNames.description: Total number of names that were screened in real-time in order to check there are correctly propagated to the cluster. Should match the 'totalNames'.

matchedNames.description: Total number of names that were successfully matched during this coverage phase. Should match the 'checkedNames'.

coverage.description: Percentage of the successfully checked names. Should normally be 100%.

items.description: List of names that were not successfully checked. Should normally be empty.

name.description: Name that failed the coverage check.

id.description: Unique identifier of the list statistics.

executionTime.description: The execution time of the list import (date-time - RFC3339).

publicationDate.description: The publication date time of the list import (date-time - RFC3339).

publicationTimestamp.description: The publication timestamp of the list, value extracted from the list or value is generated by removing one day from the import date time (date-time - RFC3339).

databaseTotalRecords.description: The count of records in the database after the import.

ListCountersDatafile.title: The statistics counter of the list for the parsing phase

invalidRecords.description: The count of invalid records. For private lists, a record may be invalid if it does not comply with the XSD or due to its contents.

ListCountersDatabase.title: The statistics counter of the list for the storing phase

purgedRecords.description: The count of purged records. In the case of a 'full' mode import, the purged records count contains the number of records not found in the datafile but present in database. These records are purged at the end of the import.

ListCounter.title: The statistics counter of the list

totalRecords.description: The count of all identified records

CoreUsage_counters.description: Counters split by product (Jetscan and Jetflow)

jetflow.description: Number of explained KYC records matches

CoreUsage_explainCounters.description: Optional additional counters which gives you the number of explain queries made in your account. This section is hidden if you do not have the explain feature enabled in your token.

clients.description: Number of screened names, by client. The keys are the client IDs sent in the requests. The '_unspecified' key is used for requests without a client ID.

projects.description: Number of screened names, by project. The keys are the project IDs sent in the requests. The '_unspecified' key is used for requests without a project ID.

avoidableWithDob.description: Number of hits which could have been discarded if a DOB was given as metadata.

avoidableWithCountry.description: Number of hits which could have been discarded if a country was given as metadata.

hitNoCountry.description: Number of hits against a watchlist record with no country information.

hitNoDob.description: Number of hits against a watchlist record with no date of birth.

hitInactive.description: Number of hits against an inactive watch list record.

hitDeceased.description: Number of hits against a deceased watch list record.

CoreSession_stats.description: Some counters about the screened records and found hits.

time.description: Timestamp taken at measure time (date-time - RFC3339).

count.description: Number of screened records since last measure.

hits.description: Number of found hits since last measure.

profileSummary.description: Add the key properties of the matched record (e.g. Main name, countries, date of birth, IDs, ...)

profileAnalytics.description: Add the additional record analytics generated by Neterium (if applicable)

matchLocation.description: Add the field path and character position where the matched text was found.

resolvedContent.description: Add the list of structured/typed content found in the screened data

screeningStats.description: Add some stats about the screening API transaction (mainly counters and perfs).

discardedMatches.description: Include the matches that have been discarded by an exception.

explanation.description: When the explainPlan feature is activated, returns a synchronous subset of the JSON explanation in the screening response.

listContext.description: Add the descriptors of all the lists involved in the screening process.

detectTitle.description: When enabled, the engine will identify any title in the passed name (mr, dr, ...). A title will have a very low weight in the final score.

detectBicCodes.description: When enabled, the engine will detect, expand and screen any Swift BIC-8 or BIC-11 in the names and texts.

useTextPlaces.description: Use the identified the city/country pairs in a screened TEXT, and automatically add them as places in the screening context. This enables the metaScore for this request only.

surnameAbbreviations.description: When enabled, the engine will accept abbreviations in the main surname words. For example, Saddam H. will match Saddam Hussein. We recommended to only turn this on for ad-hoc searches.

perfectSmallNames.description: Maximum length of words for which a perfect match is required, provided that the full name is composed by that word (and an optional legal form).

bidirectionalMatching.description: When enabled, the word-level matching will be done both from the left and right sides. This can help catching matches where the beginning of the word has 2 or more unexpected letters.

deepIndexSearch.description:
When names are screened, a word index is built for the submitted request, and list profiles are considered as candidates, i.e. potential matches, if they contain indexed words. Noise words, legal forms and other lower weighted words, are generally not indexed. Using this tweak forces the engine to index all words and hence consider any list profile that contains at least one of the words, even if the matching word is noisy.
Note that enabling this tweak can negatively impact performance.

lowQualityNicknames.description: The system contains data tables with nicknames (e.g. Anthony / Tony). When this tweak is enabled, this table will be extended with more variations like translations or language specific name alternatives (e.g. JOHN / JUAN, VIKTOR / VITYA).

anyOf.description: When enabled, the engine will return a match whenever all the words of either the screened name OR the listed name are matched, whatever the number of additional unmatched words and their position.

Configures the partial matching policy for individual names. NONE : Equivalent of false in the boolean version. Any unmatched word in the request or in the list profile will lower the score, based on its nature (e.g. a surname is more important than a middle name) BOTH: Equivalent of true in the boolean version. Assuming that the name is fully matched, any other other word in the request or the profile will only slightly impact the score. REQUEST: If all the words in the list profile are matched, any unmatched word in the request will only slightly impact the score. PROFILE: If all the words in the request are matched, any unmatched word in the profile will only slightly impact the score.   Note that, by default, at least one word in the surnames (except particles such as AL, BIN, ...) and one word in the firstnames (first/middle) should be matched. Other patterns can be configured in the partialIndividualNamePatterns tweak.

partialIndividualNamePatterns.description: List of patterns for the partial individual name matching. One pattern is in the form "x,y" where x is the minimum amount of matched words in the surnames group, and y is the minimum amount of matched words in the firstnames group (particles excluded).

anyOf.description: When enaled, a match will be considered as valid as soon as all the words in the request or in the watch list name are matched (additional or missing words have a lot less impact).

Configures the partial matching policy for entity names. NONE : Equivalent of false in the boolean version. Any unmatched word in the request or in the list profile will lower the score, based on its nature (e.g. common words like GROUP or a legal form have a lot less weight) BOTH: Equivalent of true in the boolean version. Assuming that the name is fully matched, any other other word in the request or the profile will only slightly impact the score. REQUEST: If all the words in the list profile are matched, any unmatched word in the request will only slightly impact the score. PROFILE: If all the words in the request are matched, any unmatched word in the profile will only slightly impact the score.

longAbbreviations.description: Allows a word (>3 letters) to be considered as a truncation of a much longer word (>= 6 letters). E.g. INTERN vs INTERNATIONAL.

minTextMatchLength.description: Minimum length of matches found in Texts, if only 1 word is matching.

globalLegalForms.description: Looks for legal form words in the screened entity name, whatever the places sent in the metadata (in languages that do not match the country of incorporation of the entity).

detectAttachedCountries.description: (BETA) When turned on, any risk country attached to the end of a word will be matched. This is currently only applicable for the last 5 words of a screened text.

crossBorderGeolocation.description: When comparing city data points, two cities that belong to different countries but that are within the geolocation distance will be considered as (see cityProximity option)

dob.description: Date of birth. Accepted formats are YYYY, YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY/MM/DD. (Deprecated).

items.description: Date of birth as a list of values. Accepted formats are YYYY, YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY/MM/DD.

gender.description: Gender (for individuals), if known. In case of trans gender, leave this field empty as watch list do not seem to support this value yet.

places.description: List of places related to the record. A place consist of a country and an optional city.

country.description: Written as ISO2/ISO3 code, or using the 'official' name (132 languages supported)

postalCode.description: Optional city postal code (not used yet).

addressLines.description: Address lines containing additional address information (street, number, ...)

city.description: Optional city name, which can be geolocalized.

type.description: Type of place. A place of type 'other' can match with any place found in the listed record.

ids.description: List of IDs (any type: passport, national id, driving licence, etc...

JetScanScreenRecord_metadata.description: Any other metadata key/value pairs. Check the reserved property names.

publicationDate.description: Date at which this information source was published.

source.description: Any textual description of the source.

title.description: The title of the information source, if any.

snippet.description: Short summary of the published information (vendor specific).

notes.description: Additional unstructured content about the published information.

created.description: Creation date of this profile in its list.

updated.description: Last update date for this profile in its list.

status.description: Profile status in the list: ACT (Active), INA (Inactive), DEC (Deceased)

action.description: Last action made on this profile: ADD (added), UPD (updated)

checksum.description: Profile 'footprint' (CRC) which will remain unchanged except if any information has changed.

coreChecksum.description: Profile 'footprint' (CRC) which will remain unchanged except if any 'important' information has changed. Important information includes all the properties that are part on the screening logic in a way or an other, including names, addresses, ids, dates of birth, sanctions, status, ...

inactiveDate.description: If the record is marked as inactive, the date at which it happened (if available).

sources.description: List of URLs the information's in this profile come from. You should either have an url or a source, depending on where it comes from and if the data could be successfully parsed.

legalNotice.description: Additional important legal notes delivered by the list provider

Profile_publication.description: More information about the publication of this profile.

type.description: Main type of profile: E (Entity), I (Individual)

tags.description: List of short high-level classification tags

inactiveTags.description: List of tags explicitely marked as inactive by the list provider

categories.description: List of list-specific categories for this profile

pepLevel.description: For PEPs, the highest known level of responsability

Profile_classification.description: Profile properties that help classifying the data

dates.description: Date of birth list (may be incomplete)

Profile_identity_decease.description: If this individual is deceased, more info here.

Profile_identity_countries.description: Other related countries. Some unlisted properties may be given depending on the list provider.

Profile_identity_documents.description: Known identification documents for this record.

privatisationDate.description: Date at which the company was privatized.

nationalisationDate.description: Date at which the company became nationalized.

registrationId.description: National registration ID of the company in its registration country.

taxId.description: National tax ID of the company (e.g. VAT) in its registration country.

Profile_identity_registration.description: Registration information for companies or freelance individuals. None of the properties are required and depend on the data provider.

pictures.description: List of URLs to pictures of this individual.

otherIds.description: Other known identifiers. Some unlisted properties may be given depending on the list provider.

codes.description: Other known codes. Some unlisted properties may be given depending on the list provider.

tonnage.description: For vessels, their tonnage. Given as string as the unit is sometimes given, sometimes not...

Profile_identity.description: Key identification properties of the profile.

listCategory.description: Additional category information of sanction list, if any.

issuerCode.description: Unique identifier of the sanction list provider

issuerCountry.description: ISO2 country code of the country where the sanction was issued. If 'xi', this is issued by an international organisation.

primary.description: Is this occupation the primary one of this individual?

type.description: Occupation type, as given by the list provider.

code.description: List specific code about the link.

clientReference.description: Your client ID if you need one

projectReference.description: Your project ID if you need one

type.description: Added for consistency with the jetscan API

key.description: Short ID of the list, used as prefix in the profile IDs.

description.description: More verbose description of the list.

version.description: Last time this list was updated and published by the provider (date-time - RFC3339).

timestamp.description: Last time this list was downloaded by our system (date-time - RFC3339).

snapshot.description: ID of the list snapshot taken by our system. Keep this if you want to be able to replay a request later.

legalNotice.description: Additional important legal notes given by the list provider.

strippingDetection.description: Output of the stripping detection process, if the corresponding option has been turned on.

duplicateDetection.description: Output of the duplicate detection process, if the corresponding option has been turned on.

CoreResponseItem_additionalChecks.description: Status of any additional checks performed on the submitted information

CoreResponseProfileSummary_birth.description: Places and dates of birth of individuals

matched.description: Tells if this word was actually matched or not.

matchWord.description: The word in the watch list name.

matchIndex.description: The 1-based index of the word in the name (value: 1 to the number of words of the name).

requestWord.description: When applicable, the related word in the request.

requestIndex.description: When applicable, the 1-based index of the word in the request.

score.description: Level of matching between the request word and the match word.

weight.description: The weight of the match word (if matched or missing) or request word (if unexpected) in the final score calculation.

outcome.description: Type of match detail. 'matched' if there is a match between the request word and the listed word, 'missing' if the listed word not found at all, 'mismatched' if the listed word was replaced by an other word in the request, 'unexpected' if the request word could not be linked to any word in the listed name.

flags.description: List of tags that were applied to match and used in the score calculation.

elements.description: List of scoring elements that were involed when computing the meta score. Only returned if the metaScore option has been turned on.

nameScore.description: The name matching score used as a starting value to compute the final metascore.

maxScore.description: The maximum potential metaScore, based on the matching outcome of each metadata. Actually, the maximum score is reduced whenever an element is mismatched or incomplete. For example, if the country is mismatched but the dob is perfect, this ensures that one impact does not compensate the other and results in a 100% (which is not relevant).

metaScoreImpact.description: Sum of the impact of each metascore element. This value will be added to the base name score to obtain the final metascore.

finalScore.description: Final metaScore, based on the name score and the impact brought by each metadata element.

path.description: The path of the field the data is extracted from.

countryCode.description: ISO2 country code of the place

path.description: The path of the field the data is extracted from.

countryCode.description: ISO2 country code of the place

screening.description: The service(s) processing the screening requests

discovery.description: The service(s) managing cluster status and configuration

utilities.description: The service(s) taking care of other non-screening requests

updater.description: The service(s) responsible for the watch list updates

CoreClusterStatus_services.description: Status and version of the most important running components

debtor.text: /A101020110292152 SMITH John 2021 Atwater Street Suite 216 London uk

addressLines: DUBAI INTERNET CITY SH ZAYED ROAD

object.purpose: for invoice INV123456, customer 7565443, huge car rental 2020

records.object: I met saddam hussein while I was having a drink in bagdad.

lists.name: United Nations Sanctions
lists.description: Consolidated United Nations Security Council Sanctions List

ExceptionExample1.description: Create an exception to get rid of a noisy alias.

ExceptionExample2.description: Create an exception to whitelist a precise match, in specific conditions. The checksum is part of the screening response and will allow the created exception to expire automatically if the profile changes.

ExceptionExample3.description: Create a technical exception to get rid of ANY vessel match found in the debtor's text of a transaction.

SessionCreationExample.description: Give your own reference data in your session creation request.
